title: Ton-Falk

'''Ton-Falk''' was a celestial body<ref name="SoT">[[Stay on Target (sourcebook)|''Stay on Target'']]</ref> located in the [[Ton-Falk system/Legends|Ton-Falk system]], a part of the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated on the [[Overic Griplink/Legends|Overic Griplink]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]], which linked it to the [[Florn system/Legends|Florn]] and [[Pakuuni system/Legends|Pakuuni systems]].<ref name="XimWeek" />

At some point between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="0 BBY">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates the [[Battle of Ton-Falk]] to before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which took place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Battle of Ton-Falk]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] was fought over Ton-Falk. The battle was an embarrassing defeat for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] due to the loss of an entire [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] to a small number of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and it led to [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] designing the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] for Imperial use.<ref name="SoT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ton-Falk's name was introduced via a mention of the Battle of Ton-Falk in [[Greg Gorden]]'s [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, described Ton-Falk as a location. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed.<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

[[File:Heterkus-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|250px|"Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are" placed Ton-Falk in the Heterkus sector.]]
[[Stephen J. Sansweet]]'s [[1998]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' subsequently provided the first unambiguously canonical description of Ton-Falk being a location in ''Star Wars'' Legends,<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] placed it on a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] map in grid square T-5.<ref name="TEA" /> Ultimately, [[Stay on Target (sourcebook)|''Stay on Target'']], a [[2014]] sourcebook published by [[Fantasy Flight Games]] for use with the [[Star Wars: Age of Rebellion (roleplaying game)|''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'']] line of ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying]]'' products, more firmly established Ton-Falk as a distinct celestial body.<ref name="SoT" /> "Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are" also placed Ton-Falk in the [[Heterkus sector]], but the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to ''The Essential Atlas'' overrode that placement by establishing that the Ton-Falk [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, was part of the Pakuuni [[Sector/Legends|sector]] instead.<ref name="EAOC" />
